title: rubus menkei subsp. dissociatus
intro: Rubus menkei subsp. dissociatus is a subspecies of the Rubus menkei plant, commonly known as the Menke's raspberry, a type of wild raspberry native to Eastern Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of ellagic acid and other polyphenolic compounds. Consuming Rubus menkei subsp. dissociatus has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing the risk of certain cancers, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against age-related cognitive decline. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract, and can also be used in the form of tinctures or capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
